response: Founded in 1861, the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)—ranked #1 among universities—has a student body of 11,500, employs 1,069 faculty members, and counts 98 Nobel laureates among its affiliated community. This elite, highly selective institution boasts a 3.9% acceptance rate, charges $57,590 in annual tuition, and holds a $24.6 billion endowment.
